Details: Ultraviolet irradiation (UV) and ozone treatment was reported to remove oxamyl effectively in a lab water by 83 percent [1489]. The UV and ozone unit was a component of a point-of-entry drinking water treatment system. The system consisted of a reverse osmosis (RO) module, the UV and ozone module (named as advanced simultaneous oxidation process, or ASOP), followed by a granular activated carbon (GAC) filter. The UV contact time was 33 seconds; however, the UV intensity and ozone dose were not reported. The influent to the UV and ozone module contained 1200 μg/L oxamyl with the pH level in the range of 7.0 to 8.0. The effluent oxamyl concentration from the UV and ozone unit was 210 μg/L.
